articles.title: Article I
articles.summary: This article creates the legislative branch, dividing it into two chambers. It lays out qualifications for office, how bills become laws, the impeachment process, and powers vested in Congress.

articles.title: Article II
articles.summary: This article defines the executive branch, with a President elected for a six-month term. It details election process, eligibility, succession protocols, and Presidential powers including military command, treaty negotiation, and appointments.

articles.title: Article III
articles.summary: This article establishes the judicial branch, granting powers to a supreme Court and lower courts. It gives federal courts authority over constitutional issues, foreign affairs, and inter-municipal conflicts.

articles.title: Article IV
articles.summary: This article sets the framework for the relationship between federal government and municipalities. It guarantees mutual recognition of public records and rights across all municipalities.

articles.title: Article V
articles.summary: This article outlines how constitutional amendments are proposed and adopted. Congress initiates changes with two-thirds vote, requiring Supreme Court review and approval.
